characters.name: Aunt Clotilda
characters.description: Aunt ClotildaisHarrietteandRachel's deceased great aunt. Upon hearing about of their great-aunt Clotilda's untimely death, Harriette and Rachel were tasked with scattering her ashes in Lake Michigan. However, Carl accidentally mistakes her urn for a candy tin and dumps Clotilda's ashes in a hefty bag. He realizes his mistake when Harriette reveals to him it's an urn wit their great-aunt's ashes in them. Convincing them into letting him come and leaving the urn with him, Carl manages to cover for his mistake by not only putting all of Clotilda's ashes in the urn, but also a mixture of a fire log. He soon apologizes to her ashes for his mistake. However, Harriette finds out and punishes Carl by making him take her out to dinner at Chez Josephine's.

characters.name: Baby Girl
characters.description: Baby GirlFriends:RenaldoChantaeDornita3J(formerly)Enemies:Steve Urkel3JRichie CrawfordFirst appearance:My Big BrotherLast appearance:Karate KidsPortrayer:Natalie DeselleMaya Lot Baby Girl,played by Natalie Deselle (and later by Maya Lott), is a female tough thug who hangs out with her equally tough friends Chante and Dornita. She first appeared in season 7'sMy Big Brother(in honor of the Big Brothers/Big Sisters Association). WhenUrkelshowed up to3J's part of the neighborhood to confront him about his inability to read, she and her friends gave him a hard time. He was only saved by 3J whom happened to be friends with Baby Girl, Chante and Dornita. He gave the girls $50 in order to leave Steve alone, unaware that he took the money out of their pockets and gave it to them as his own.

characters.name: Big Daddy Urkel
characters.description: Amos "Big Daddy" UrkelBirth:January 3, 1950Age:72Immediate Family:see:FamilyMyrtle Urkel(daughter)Unnamed WifeRelatives:Steve Urkel(nephew)Herb Urkel(brother)Diane Urkel(sister-in-law)Uncle Ernie Urkel(brother)Uncle Cecil Urkel(brother)Aunt Muriel Urkel(younger Sister)Uncle Omar Urkel( younger brother)Aunt Oona Urkel(Younger Sister; Deceased)Dirk Urkel(brother)Cornelius Eugene Urkel(nephew)Julie Urkel(niece)Cleotus Urkel(nephew)Unnamed fatherUnnamed motherLaura Winslow(niece-in-law)Friends:Laura WinslowCarl WinslowEddie WinslowFirst appearance:Nightmare at Urkel OaksCousin Urkel(mentioned)Last appearance:Dumb Belle of the BallPortrayer:Reginald VelJohnson Amos "Big Daddy" Urkelis the wealthy father ofMyrtle Mae Urkeland the uncle ofSteve Urkel. He was played byReginald VelJohnson. Big Daddy Urkel is a rich peanut farmer from Biloxi, Mississippi who made his fortune selling peanut products, with Steve claiming that he earns a penny for each time someone eat one of his peanuts. He was mentioned by Myrtle that she was sent to live with Herb as an exchange inCousin Urkel. Big Daddy was mentioned by Steve inHave Yourself a Merry Winslow Christmasthat he like the rest of his relatives all pay him bribe money to never visit them due to his unintended clumsiness. Big Daddy Urkel is a rich peanut farmer from Biloxi, Mississippi who made his fortune selling peanut products, with Steve claiming that he earns a penny for each time someone eat one of his peanuts. He was mentioned by Myrtle that she was sent to live with Herb as an exchange inCousin Urkel. Big Daddy was mentioned by Steve inHave Yourself a Merry Winslow Christmasthat he like the rest of his relatives all pay him bribe money to never visit them due to his unintended clumsiness. He was mention a couple of time until he made his first appearance (Via Dream) inNightmare at Urkel Oakswhere upon seeing Myrtle trying to have her way with Eddie, thinking otherwise and plans to hurt Eddie himself, only for Myrtle to tell him she loves Eddie and he told her to wait for him in the helicopter. Later after explaining to Eddie how he wants his daughter to be happy, and to do that he wants Eddie to marry Myrtle which he refuse at first until Big Daddy offered him $10 Million and Eddie gave in. Later at Urkel Oaks, Eddie was confuse that Big Daddy was able to pay for everything for the wedding, despite being a Peanut farmer until Big Daddy explain that he make all sorts of Peanut Products. During the Ceremony Big Daddy made an announcement which had him and the rest of the Urkel family cheering that Myrtle is getting married, However just before Eddie and Myrtle were declared Husband and Wife (with Big Daddy crossing his fingers) Greta appeared and told everyone this wedding is a farce due to Big Daddy paying Eddie to marry Myrtle. He then Witness Greta and Myrtle duking it out as they destroyed the buffet and nearly tore Eddie apart, resulting in the dream ending. InDumb Belle of the Ball, Big Daddy had no r

characters.name: Bob Faldo
characters.description: Bob dancing with Laura Robert "Bob" Faldo (Bobaloba-Ding-Dong) was Waldo Faldo’s cousin. He was a little person who made his only appearance during “They Shoot Urkels, Don't They?” He appeared as Laura’s blind date during the Dance Marathon hosted by Estelle and Fletcher.

characters.name: Boyd Higgins
characters.description: Boyd Higgins,also known asBuck Higgins,is one of characters in the showFamily Matters. In his first meeting, Buck appears at the Corner Pocket to be good natured and friendly toEddieandRodney. Boyd challenges Eddie to a friendly match of pool. However,Stevesenses suspicious motives on Higgins' part due to his uncle,Cecil, recognizing Buck from another pool hall he played at. Urkel tries to warn Eddie against playing Buck as he is a renowned pool hustler, only for him to ignore it and lose the bet. Higgins' true personality shows and he threatens Eddie with bodily harm if he's not paid the $250. Returning to the Corner Pocket, Eddie gives Buck the money. However, then Urkel challenges him to a double or nothing match-up. Boyd is insulted when he calls the latter out for being scared of an extra money challenge. Higgins accepts the bet against Steve. To Buck and everyone else's surprise, Urkel shows a talent in pool. He was close to winning until Higgins cheats by breaking his glasses. Eddie tries to stick up for Steve by pointing out that he was cheating and that's unacceptable. However, a menacing threat from Boyd puts him back in his place. Carl comes in just in time with an equally imposingEstelleand saves Urkel by forcing his thugs back inside. After learning what Buck has done and with $500 on the line, he announces that he will fill in for Steve. Boyd claimed it's not fair but Carl tells him off that his cheating by breaking Urkel's glasses wasn't either. He also mentions he has looked around enough to find a couple of laws being broken in The Corner Pocket and that Buck was responsible for doing such. Carl gives him an ultimatum: allow him to fill in for Steve or else he will have the latter arrested and escorted to Cook County Jail. Buck reluctantly and wisely allows Carl to fill in for Steve. He subsequently loses the game after Carl and Mother Winslow takes them back to school about pool.

characters.name: Bruce Lee Urkel
characters.description: Bruce Lee UrkelFamily:Herb Urkel(father)Diane Urkel(mother)Friends:Laura WinslowCarl WinslowRichie CrawfordJerry Jamal JamesonEnemies:NinjasJack and his GangThe SerpentsThe PiranhasFirst appearance:The Substitute SonLast appearance:Karate KidsPortrayer:Jaleel WhiteBruce Lee Urkelis an alter-ego created by Steve via a celebrity sample potion called Bruce Juice in Season 6'sThe Substitute Son. He was portrayed byJaleel White. "Boards don't fight back" ~Bruce Lee Urkel When Laura tried to convince him to use Stefan, Urkel refuses. He tells her that he won't be enough to protect her from the thugs who harassed her at a longshoreman's bar and used the transformation chamber to become Bruce Lee Urkel. After a lengthy fight, he emerged victorious against the thugs over there. When Laura tried to convince him to use Stefan, Urkel refuses. He tells her that he won't be enough to protect her from the thugs who harassed her at a longshoreman's bar and used the transformation chamber to become Bruce Lee Urkel. After a lengthy fight, he emerged victorious against the thugs over there. He makes his second appearance inRandom Acts Of Science, when Steve teamed up with Carl to defeat a notorious gang known as the Serpents who raids police storages left unprotected. Tricking the gang into letting them use the transformation chamber, both Carl and Steve emerge as both Bruce Lee Urkel andBruce Lee Winslow. They subsequently defeat The Serpents and knock them unconscious long enough for the police to arrive and arrest them. Bruce Lee Urkel makes his final appearance inKarate Kids, after Urkel learns that the Piranhas have threatened both Richie and 3J, he plots for payback. Using his transformation chamber, they become their Bruce Lee counterparts to defeat the Piranhas and take back the park for the public. The character was written out of the show after this episode.

characters.name: Bruce Lee Winslow
characters.description: Bruce Lee WinslowFriends:Bruce Lee UrkelEnemies:NinjasThe SerpentsFirst appearance:Random Acts of ScienceLast appearance:Random Acts of SciencePortrayer:Reginald VelJohnson Bruce Lee Winslow(played byReginald VelJohnson) is the Bruce Lee alter-ego ofCarl Winslow. He only appeared inRandom Acts of Science. He teamed up withBruce Lee Urkelto defeat a dangerous gang known The Serpents, whom has been raiding and stealing from police warehouses. Though they made a mess of the warehouse, both Bruce Lee Urkel and Winslow was able to defeat The Serpents and knock them out cold.

characters.name: Buddy Goodrich
characters.description: Buddy Goodrich,played by Ron Glass, is a comedian and actor who appears in the episodeFalse Arrestand has fans such as the Winslows who watch his show on television every week. However,Carlis the only one who isn't impressed by him as he thinks Buddy is a waste of time because of his bad way of parenting his adopted sons on the show. When Carl goes to the venue where Buddy's upcoming one-man show is going to be taped, Carl notices Buddy's car parked in the handicapped spot. He asks a stagehand to tell Buddy to move his car, who points to Buddy's dressing for Carl. Buddy's assistant appears and Carl asks to speak to Buddy about the parking spot. Buddy soon appears signing autographs, then gives the assistant his book after Carl asks a second time. Buddy refuses to move the car, then proceeds to provoke Carl three times despite his warnings to stop and he's arrested for it. Carl reads him his rights. The next day, Buddy is released from jail and scheduled for trial. He reveals on television that the "rogue" cop arrested him when he "didn't provoke" him (in actuality he did provoke Carl that lead to his arrest). The kids get mad and don't want to talk to Carl, even when he maintained he did the right thing. Buddy visits the Winslow home to try to get out of paying for the fine, but Carl refuses to budge on the matter. Carl orders him to leave the house at once after explaining why he refuses to cave in to Buddy's requests. However, when Buddy says that he's got a good lawyer and is confident of winning the case, the Winslows, having secretly listened to his confession, become angry about it. Realizing that Carl was right in his actions, they congratulate him for telling Goodrich off. Buddy is found guilty after his assistant testifies against him in court two weeks later and is forced pay a big fine and spend 100 hours picking up garbage off the interstate. Carl, Harriette, Estelle, and Rachel take a slow drive on the interstate to humiliate him more. Buddy hasn't been seen since, possibly because his acting career and reputation were tarnished after everyone learned that he provoked Carl.

characters.name: Carl Winslow
characters.description: Carl Otis WinslowBirth:Carlton Otis WinslowJune 18, 1953Age:33 (season 1)34 (season 2)35 (season 3)36 (season 4)37 (season 5)38 (season 6)39 (season 7)40 (season 8)41 (season 9)Address:1516 W. Wrightwood AvenueChicago, IllinoisOccupation:Police OfficerNickname(s):Big Guy (bySteve Urkel)Immediate Family:see:FamilyHarriette Winslow(wife)Eddie Winslow(son)Laura Winslow(older daughter)Judy Winslow(younger daughter)Samuel Winslow(father; deceased)Estelle Winslow(mother; deceased )Calvin Winslow(younger brother)Darryl Winslow(oldest brother)Frank Winslow(younger brother)Walter Winslow(younger brother)Fletcher Thomas(stepfather)Jerry Jamal Jameson(adopted son)Relatives:Rachel Crawford(sister-in-law)Richie Crawford(nephew)Jimmy Baines(father-in-law)Darlene Baines(mother-in-law)Clarence(cousin-in-law)Friends:Steve UrkelStefan UrquelleMaxine JohnsonWaldo Geraldo FaldoMyra MonkhouseEnemies:Steve Urkel(occasionally)Charlie Carnelli(Season 2)Nick Neidermeyer(Seasons 7 & 8)First appearance:The Mama Who Came to DinnerLast appearance:Lost in Space (Part 2)Portrayer:Reginald VelJohnsonCarlton "Carl" Otis Winslowis the protagonist, later Deteuragonist portrayed byReginald VelJohnsonon the ABC/CBS American sitcomFamily Matters. June 18, 1953 Charlie Carnelli(Season 2)Nick Neidermeyer(Seasons 7 & 8) Carl is a police officer for the Chicago Police Department.  and then as a police sergeant, next Lieutenant and then Captain. Carl takes his role as the bread-winner of the household very seriously. His opinion of being the "man of the house" tends to get in the way of more personal issues with his wife, Harriette. His ego often gets him into trouble with Harriette, although he is somewhat quick to concede to his wife, as she often ends up teaching him a lesson when it comes to resolving petty marital disputes. Carl can be very cheap, he usually cut corners to save money or when he sees the bills and tries to pays his own taxes. He even builds things himself, when he sees the bill of how much it would it cost (understandably) and decide to build it himself. That ends in disaster and Harriette having to see his brother, Calvin to help them properly with their taxes. Carl has a tendency to come down very hard on his only son,Eddie, and occasionally his pesky neighborSteve, while being protective of his daughters,LauraandJudy, at the same time. On occasion, Carl acts in an erratic fashion and usually blames it on his stressful job. However, deep down, Carl has shown he is a very loving and caring father. Once, he became offended when Laura wore a skimpy, revealing dress inDumb Belle of the Balland gave Eddie an ultimatum when it was revealed that he was drinking beers at his fraternity parties inTips for a Better Life. InHot Stuff, this had Eddie telling Carl "It's time you stopped acting like a cop and started acting like my dad!". His favorite food is doughnuts, something very stereotypical of policemen. Carl is the son of Estelle and Samuel Winslow. According to the pilo

Wrightwood AvenueChicago, IllinoisOccupation:Police OfficerNickname(s):Big Guy (bySteve Urkel)Immediate Family:see:FamilyHarriette Winslow(wife)Eddie Winslow(son)Laura Winslow(older daughter)Judy Winslow(younger daughter)Samuel Winslow(father; deceased)Estelle Winslow(mother; deceased )Calvin Winslow(younger brother)Darryl Winslow(oldest brother)Frank Winslow(younger brother)Walter Winslow(younger brother)Fletcher Thomas(stepfather)Jerry Jamal Jameson(adopted son)Relatives:Rachel Crawford(sister-in-law)Richie Crawford(nephew)Jimmy Baines(father-in-law)Darlene Baines(mother-in-law)Clarence(cousin-in-law)Friends:Steve UrkelStefan UrquelleMaxine JohnsonWaldo Geraldo FaldoMyra MonkhouseEnemies:Steve Urkel(occasionally)Charlie Carnelli(Season 2)Nick Neidermeyer(Seasons 7 & 8)First appearance:The Mama Who Came to DinnerLast appearance:Lost in Space (Part 2)Portrayer:Reginald VelJohnsonCarlton "Carl" Otis Winslowis the protagonist, later Deteuragonist portrayed byReginald VelJohnsonon the ABC/CBS American sitcomFamily Matters. June 18, 1953 Charlie Carnelli(Season 2)Nick Neidermeyer(Seasons 7 & 8) Carl is a police officer for the Chicago Police Department.  and then as a police sergeant, next Lieutenant and then Captain. Carl takes his role as the bread-winner of the household very seriously. His opinion of being the "man of the house" tends to get in the way of more personal issues with his wife, Harriette. His ego often gets him into trouble with Harriette, although he is somewhat quick to concede to his wife, as she often ends up teaching him a lesson when it comes to resolving petty marital disputes. Carl can be very cheap, he usually cut corners to save money or when he sees the bills and tries to pays his own taxes. He even builds things himself, when he sees the bill of how much it would it cost (understandably) and decide to build it himself. That ends in disaster and Harriette having to see his brother, Calvin to help them properly with their taxes. Carl has a tendency to come down very hard on his only son,Eddie, and occasionally his pesky neighborSteve, while being protective of his daughters,LauraandJudy, at the same time. On occasion, Carl acts in an erratic fashion and usually blames it on his stressful job. However, deep down, Carl has shown he is a very loving and caring father. Once, he became offended when Laura wore a skimpy, revealing dress inDumb Belle of the Balland gave Eddie an ultimatum when it was revealed that he was drinking beers at his fraternity parties inTips for a Better Life. InHot Stuff, this had Eddie telling Carl "It's time you stopped acting like a cop and started acting like my dad!". His favorite food is doughnuts, something very stereotypical of policemen. Carl is the son of Estelle and Samuel Winslow. According to the pilo

characters.name: Carl Winslow/Nerd Carl
characters.description: Carl WinslowFirst appearance:"To Be or Not to Be (Part 1)"Last appearance:"To Be or Not to Be (Part 2)"Portrayer:Reginald VelJohnsonNerd Carl, also referred to as"Narl", is a nerdy alter-ego ofCarl Winslowthat was created when Carl used theUrkel Transformation Chamberin "To Be or Not to Be (Part 1)", unaware that it had been tampered with byMyra Monkhouse. The alter-ego disappeared in "To Be or Not to Be (Part 2)" when the chamber was fixed. He was the unwitting victim ofMyra's after she tampered with the Transformation Chamber thinkingStefanwill use it to return asSteveand she can ensnare him. She soon realized her mistake and told Stefan to stop the chamber as she rewired the chamber and changed the setting from suave to nerd. Sure enough, the chamber is busted and Carl comes out as a nerd version of himself, complete with pocket protector and his boxers sticking out of his pants. It was soon revealed that due to the interrupted process, only half the Urkel personality took hold, as at certain points Carl's former personality makes a breakthrough, or as Stefan put it "he is half-nerd, half-Carl". Carl spends the next few days walking a mile in Urkel's shoes, unwittingly being clumsy and enjoying things that Steve might like or does like, such as listening to the Bulgarian Women's choir, eating cheese and ironing his underwear. This caused the nerd genes in his body to accelerate rapidly.

characters.name: Cassie Lynn
characters.description: Cassie Lynn Nubbleswas the nemesis of ofLaura WinslowandSteve Urkel. Cassie Lynn Nubbles is labeled as the mean girl of Vanderbilt High School. She was portayed byKim Valentine. Cassie was introduced in the episode "Making the Team" where she was shown to be the head cheerleader at Vanderbilt High School. When Laura tries out for the cheerleading squad, she clashes with Cassie when Laura wanted to change some of the cheers. During the basketball game, Cassie Lynn refuses to cheer for the basketball team because she finds basketball boring, but Laura is able to energize the crowd with the cheers that she made up that helps the team win. After this, Cassie Lynn is kicked off the cheering squad and this made her hate Laura more.

characters.name: Charles Mallory
characters.description: Charles Mallorywas an innocent victim whom was shot a convenience store gunman and whose death haunted Carl inI Should Have Done Something. Not much was known about Mr. Mallory's life except he left behind his wife,Helen, and their dog. He went to a store to pick up some ice cream for a movie they were watching and became a victim of a robber armed with a gun. Though Carl was commended for his efforts in apprehending the gunman, he was unable to save Mr. Mallory's life and therefore took his anger out on his family. It took visiting his grave and talking to Helen that finally helped Carl come to terms with the hostage situation gone wrong.

characters.name: Clarence
characters.description: Clarence BainesBirth:Clarence BainesApril 16 1970'sAge:52Address:Detroit, MINickname(s):Easy CFamily:Unnamed grandmother unnamed grandfather unnamed grandmother unnamed grandfatherImmediate Family:see:FamilyMother: HelenUnnamed FatherRelatives:Harriette Winslow(cousin)Rachel Crawford(cousin)Carl Winslow(cousin-in-law)Robert Crawford(cousin-in-law; deceased)Jimmy Baines(uncle deceased)Darlene Baines(aunt)Eddie Winslow(second cousin)Laura Winslow(second cousin)Judy Winslow(second cousin)Richie Crawford(second cousin)Steve Urkel(second cousin-in-law)Greta McClure(second cousin-in-law)First appearance:Jailhouse BluesLast appearance:Jailhouse BluesPortrayer:Shaun BakerClarence Baines (Easy C)isEddie,LauraandJudy's 2nd cousin. He is alsoHarrietteandRachel's first cousin once removed. He lives in Detroit and makes his only appearance inJailhouse Blues. The Winslows stated that the last time they saw Clarence, he was a cute little boy with good manners, described as a little "angel". However, when he arrives, he is all but the opposite described. His mother stated that she felt Clarence was falling into the wrong crowd and thought that sending him to Chicago could do him some good and reform him. Later, Clarence's true colors are revealed after he "borrows" a red Ferrari with his female friends. He tempts Eddie into going with him and kidnaps Steve so he can't squeal on him.

characters.name: Cleotus Urkel
characters.description: Cleotus Urkelis the cousin ofSteve Urkel. There was no mention of Cleotus's early life because Steve never mentioned him. It was implied that inHot Rods to Heck, he used to be a drag racer before his injury. InNightmare at Urkel Oaks, Cleotus was briefly seen at Myrtle and Eddie's wedding, where he work in hisAunt Muriel's candle shop.

characters.name: Cornelius Eugene Urkel
characters.description: Cornelius Eugene (OGD) UrkelBirth:Cornelius Eugene Urkel1970'sAddress:Detroit, MIImmediate Family:see:FamilyUnknown parentsUnknown grandmotherUnknown GrandfatherRelatives:Herb Urkel(uncle)Diane Urkel(aunt)Steve Urkel(cousin)Myrtle Mae Urkel(cousin)Uncle Ernie Urkel(uncle)Uncle Cecil Urkel(uncle)Big Daddy Urkel(uncle)Aunt Muriel Urkel(aunt)Uncle Omar Urkel(uncle)Aunt Oona Urkel(aunt)Omar UrkelJulie Urkel(cousin)Dirk Urkel(uncle)Cleotus Urkel(cousin)Laura Winslow(cousin-in-law)Friends:Carl WinslowEddie WinslowLaura WinslowJerry Jamal JamesonEnemies:Stefan Urquelle(formerly)Fresh SqueezeSteve Urkel (formerly)First appearance:Original Gangsta DawgLast appearance:Original Gangsta DawgPortrayer:Jaleel WhiteCornelius Eugene Urkel, (akaOGD) portrayed by Jaleel White, isSteveandMyrtle's cousin from Detroit. In his first appearance of season 9'sOriginal Gangsta Dawg, it was revealed in an early scene that Steve refuses to give him money, even though he was in serious trouble with a tough, tall loan shark named Fresh Squeeze (played by Mongo Brownlee) and tries to avoid him. It is revealed from Cornelius that he had (apparently in a legal way) loaned him some cash to buy a new stereo and that he paid him back with 20% interest but now Fresh Squeeze wants 600% (possibly to make a few extra purchases). Urkel is not convinced and mentions that the thug is a loan shark who is ripping Cornelius off. He mentions that OGD should have gone to the police in Detroit and report this. He refuses knowing how power hungry those cops in his hometown are and wants Urkel to loan him the money. Before leaving to be with their uncle on a nuclear-powered submarine vessel, Steve warns him never to return to the Winslow home while he's gone. Cornelius, having ignored Steve's warning, returns anyway and impressesEddieand3Jbut his behavior disgustsCarl,HarrietteandLaura. Cornelius gives 3J a rap CD, although the lyrics clearly disrespect police officers. This enrages Carl because he's a proud officer of the law. Some time later, Harriette learns from the grandmother whom Cornelius lives with that he's a good kid but his hate for the police both had something to with the people he hangs out with and where he's from. She tells Carl he can change his opinion about the police by giving him a positive outlook on them. He's worried that Cornelius will be a bad influence on 3J and Richie. Harriette tells him that they can only hope they rub off on him first beforehand. Later on at the house, Carl was confronted by Fresh Squeeze and tricks the thug in letting him accompany him to where OGD is when instead taking him to the station. At the Sizzle Club after Missy Elliot's performance, he was seen trying to hit on Laura and makes a nasty comment toStefancausing him to call Steve in the nuclear submarine's control room. During a conference call between Steve, Stefan and Cornelius, Steve warns him to return to Detroit at once, and Stefan warns him that he could go in a box or a bus - it's his

characters.name: Daniel Wallace
characters.description: Daniel Wallaceis an ex-boyfriend ofMaxine JohnsonandLaura Winslow. His skill is sweet-talking girls before being able to have sex with them, and then he dumps them (similar toDerekinAll The Wrong Moves). He tried to talk Maxine into having sex with him right before he dumped her. Later on, he did the same thing to Laura. He was portrayed by Bumper Robinson who earlier played another friend and bandmate of Eddie's named Kyle inRock Video.

characters.name: Dave McClure
characters.description: Dave L. McClureAge:UnknownImmediate Family:see:FamilyGreta McClure(daughter)Unnamed WifeEnemies:Eddie WinslowFirst appearance:My BodyguardLast appearance:Getting BuffMentioned OnlyPortrayer:Ron Canada David L. "Dave" McClureisGreta’sfather andEddie’s former boss. He owns multipleMighty Weeniefast food restaurants. He hates Eddie because he dates his daughter. In the episodeGetting Buff, he cuts off Greta after he gave her an ultimatum of whether to break up with Eddie or have her money taken away. They haven't made amends for years. .

characters.name: Derick Benner
characters.description: Derick BennerOccupation:VP/Head of Sales at Ferguson's (formerly)Nickname(s):Mr. BennerFirst appearance:A Pain in HarassmentLast appearance:A Pain in HarassmentPortrayer:Mark Linn-Baker Mr. Derick Benneris a minor character played by Mark Linn-Baker in A Pain in Harassment. He was the former head sales buyer at Fergusons until he was fired and replaced by Harriet.  While it's said he's tough with his employees and makes them work harder using the coach-and-players analogy, he’s actually shown to be a mean-spirited and verbally abusive bully to them and only cares about making as much money as possible.
characters.personality_section: While it's said he's tough with his employees and makes them work harder using the coach-and-players analogy, he’s actually shown to be a mean-spirited and verbally abusive bully to them and only cares about making as much money as possible.

characters.name: Dexter Thornhill
characters.description: Dexter ThornhillAge:UnknownEnemies:Steve UrkelFirst appearance:Presumed UrkelLast appearance:Presumed UrkelPortrayer:Robert LaughlinDexter Thornhill, played by Robert Laughlin in his only appearance, is Steve's rival similar toCassie Lynn Nubbleswas toLaura Winslow. He was a nerd who went out of his way to outdo him in everything. His only appearance was in Season 5's "Presumed Urkel", where Steve is accused of blowing up the school's science lab just before the State science fair that he and Thornhill were to take part in. In the upcoming student trial where Steve is to be expelled from school if found guilty, Thornhill is the prosecutor and calls in the science teacher, Mr. Sweeney, and Carl. However, the former states while the last person he saw in his room before the explosion was Steve, he never actually saw the explosion and thus, doesn't know who really caused it. Carl states that while Steve has done catastrophic damage in his house numerous times, he never denied causing any of them like he did with the lab explosion and had saved his life before. After calling Steve and the school custodian, Mr. Looney, who was in the room with Steve right before the explosion, Laura, acting as the defense, sees how desperate Thornhill is in trying to get Steve expelled and calls him to the stand. With a blacklight lantern, she was able to prove his guilt by making him reveal chemical stains on his hands, having gotten them on there when he burnt them and while trying to move the hot beaker, before adding extra explosive chemicals in Urkel's graffiti solvent and turning the bunsen burner up high. Dexter loses it and confesses to his crime, stating that he deserved to have won 1st prize in the science fair years ago. At the end of his confession, he says was tired of staying up into the early morning hours, while Steve only glances at a page and gets straight-As and condemns him for being better than him. After the charges against Steve are dropped, Dexter was expelled and likely arrested for his crime in destroying the lab and incriminating Steve.

characters.name: Diane Urkel
characters.description: Diane Roberta UrkelisSteve Urkel's mother. When Diane gave birth to Steve, he tried to push him back inside of her. She has six brothers-in-law and two sisters-in-law. Her nieces include Myrtle and Julie, while her nephews are Cleotus and Cornelius (OGD). She, like her husband, has a lot of hate and contempt for Steve. Her voice was heard in "Pulling Teeth", asking Steve why he isn't in bed, to which Steve replies he is waiting for the call. Annoyed, she orders him to go to bed at once.

characters.name: Dirk Urkel
characters.description: Colonel Dirk Urkel is a character from Family matters who works at The Pentagon and was first mentioned in Season 2 episode 10 "The Science Project". He is only mentioned and never seen physically in the show. He is Steve Urkel's Uncle. Season 2 episode 10 "The Science Project"

characters.description: Grabowski is Doctor in episode Out with the Old.

characters.name: Eddie Urkel
characters.description: Eddie UrkelBirth:Edward James Arthur UrkelJanuary 28, 1974Address:Chicago, ILImmediate Family:see:FamilyCarl Urkel(father)Harriette Baines-Urkel (mother)Laura Urkel(younger sister)Judith "Judy" Urkel (sister, youngest)Enemies:Steve UrkelFirst appearance:What Do You Know?Last appearance:What Do You Know?Portrayer:Darius McCraryEdward James Arthur "Eddie" Urkel(born January 28, 1974) played by Darius McCrary, is the nerdy alter-ego form ofEddie Winslow. Eddie got an invitation on the game show "What Do You Know?" and wanted Steve to go in his place, hoping to use his unlimited knowledge to clean up on the show. Urkel refused to cooperate and told him to go study for the show instead. However, Eddie refused because he had to be at the studio the next day. Eddie decides to pay him back by going into the transformation chamber and became Eddie Urkel. Hoping to use his unlimited genius to clean up on the show, Eddie learns his lesson the hard way about studying and working hard for it when Steve comes on the show as a contestant to stop him. They matched wits for a while in the scientific and other intellectual categories that made the third contestant, Lyle, feel inadequate. By the end of the show, both Stevie and Eddie have 3970 points while Lyle got only 10. However, they answered the game's bonus question, which asked for the name of a model who appeared in a recent Sports Illustrated swimsuit magazine, incorrectly and bet all of their points, resulting in Lyle winning.
